title: The One Where The Stripper Cries

jokes.text: Fish. Seaweed. A sunken ship. - Things you find in the ocean.
jokes.setup: Joey is excitedly announcing he's going to be on a game show
jokes.punchline: The friends immediately demonstrate Pyramid gameplay, revealing Joey's going on this specific show
jokes.explanation: Well-constructed reveal that shows rather than tells. Good economy and natural character interaction.

jokes.text: Yeah. Sorry, boys, this ride's closing.
jokes.setup: Discussing Phoebe's bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: Phoebe makes a sexual innuendo about her upcoming marriage
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe innuendo, well-delivered but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Ross and I always wanted to be Donny and Marie. You guys just keep getting cooler and cooler.
jokes.setup: Ross admits he and Chandler wanted to be Donny and Marie Osmond
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic response highlighting how uncool this makes them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler sarcasm, well-integrated with character dynamics.

jokes.text: Oh, God, that's right. I blocked that out.
jokes.setup: Ross mentions they used to perform for family and friends
jokes.punchline: Monica's horrified reaction revealing the performances were traumatically bad
jokes.explanation: Efficient callback to sibling embarrassment, good character consistency.

jokes.text: I'm leaving you.
jokes.setup: Chandler's continued enthusiasm about Donny and Marie
jokes.punchline: Monica's deadpan threat to leave him over this embarrassing revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with excellent economy. Strong character moment.

jokes.text: Did I go to this school?
jokes.setup: Ross rattling off obscure college connections and relationships
jokes.punchline: Chandler's bewildered question about whether he even attended the same school
jokes.explanation: Great economy and relatable observation about social memory differences.

jokes.text: You're right. By saying 'nice,' I'm virtually licking her.
jokes.setup: Ross warning Chandler about commenting on another woman while married to Monica
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic escalation of how innocent his comment was
jokes.explanation: Excellent Chandler sarcasm with unexpected escalation. Quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Thanks to a little something called Helvetica Bold 24-point.
jokes.setup: College Ross proudly showing off band fliers he made
jokes.punchline: His overly technical and nerdy explanation of the font choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect college Ross nerdiness. Very character-specific and authentic to the era.

jokes.text: Man, we're gonna rock that Asian Student Union.
jokes.setup: The guys are excited about their band's upcoming gig
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're only playing for a small, specific campus group
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of their rock star fantasies with reality check.

jokes.text: We're called Way/No Way. - No way. - Way.
jokes.setup: Ross announces their band name
jokes.punchline: Immediate demonstration of how the name works in conversation
jokes.explanation: Clever demonstration of the band name's interactive nature. Well-structured.

jokes.text: Fresh. Boss. Mint.
jokes.setup: The guys trying to sound cool in the 80s
jokes.punchline: String of dated slang terms that sound ridiculous now
jokes.explanation: Good period humor showing how they tried to be cool. Moderately effective.

jokes.text: I mean, we can get laid any time we want. Totally. - I had sex in high school. - Me too. I'm good at it.
jokes.setup: The guys making a pact to not pursue the same girl
jokes.punchline: Their transparent lies about their sexual prowess
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing their insecurity masked as bravado.

jokes.text: That's Missy Goldberg, Phoebe Cates and Molly Ringwald... who we can't go out with. - Those are the pacts. And Sheena Easton. But we probably couldn't get her anyway. Maybe not you.
jokes.setup: Making pacts about girls they can't date
jokes.punchline: Including impossibly famous celebrities, then Chandler's dig at Ross
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and period references with nice character dig at the end.

jokes.text: Your hands are shaking. - I know. And I can't stop sweating.
jokes.setup: Chandler approaching Missy to ask her out
jokes.punchline: His physical manifestation of nervousness
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing nervousness. Relatable but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I know it can be intimidating for regular people to be around celebrities... but relax. I'm just like you, only better-looking and richer.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to reassure his Pyramid partner
jokes.punchline: His backhanded compliment that makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey obliviousness. Great character integration and structure.

jokes.text: I see what I did there. Okay, okay. I'm writing in my... - Diary. - No, more like a notebook.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to give clues about 'line' items
jokes.punchline: His meta-commentary on his own mistakes and continued failure
jokes.explanation: Good Joey confusion and self-awareness moment. Solid character comedy.

jokes.text: If I'm building a house, the plan... isn't called a 'shmooprint'... I can't do that either? In high school, I once had sex with a girl right in the middle of the...? - Cafeteria? - Yeah, but they're not looking for that.
jokes.setup: Joey struggling with Pyramid clues
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate personal anecdote as a clue attempt
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey moment - inappropriate oversharing in wrong context. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: What? - The strippers and the guys dancing... and, you know, pee-pees flying about.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking about the bachelorette party activities
jokes.punchline: Her innocent but graphic description of male strippers
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe innocent-yet-crude language. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: Oh, okay. I see what you're doing. That's fine. This is all there is. Just tea. Okay. Raunchy.
jokes.setup: Being told there won't be strippers at her bachelorette party
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic 'raunchy' comment about tea and elegant party setup
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery with great economy. Good character moment.

jokes.text: I've got a big wad of ones in my purse.
jokes.setup: Phoebe's disappointment about the tame party
jokes.punchline: Revealing she came prepared with stripper tip money
jokes.explanation: Good reveal showing her expectations. Efficient and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: and get an ETA on the pee-pees.
jokes.setup: Rachel agreeing to check with Monica about party plans
jokes.punchline: Using Phoebe's ridiculous terminology for male anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good callback with efficient delivery. Works well in context.

jokes.text: You put this in your coffee. - A spoon. Your hands. Your face.
jokes.setup: Gene giving clues about cream/milk for coffee
jokes.punchline: Joey's increasingly absurd and literal guesses
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity from Joey. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: It's white. - Paper. Snow. A ghost!
jokes.setup: Another clue about white things
jokes.punchline: Joey's pattern of random, unrelated guesses including 'ghost'
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Joey's random guessing pattern. 'Ghost' is particularly absurd.

jokes.text: It's made from eggs. - Chickens?
jokes.setup: Clue about mayonnaise (made from eggs)
jokes.punchline: Joey's backwards logic thinking chickens are made from eggs
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey logic fail. Great economy and perfect character stupidity.

jokes.text: I got a kid starting college. I have to get surgery on my knee. You just lost me 10 grand.
jokes.setup: Joey's poor Pyramid performance
jokes.punchline: Gene's increasingly desperate personal stakes revelation
jokes.explanation: Good escalating stakes that make Joey's failure more uncomfortable and funny.

jokes.text: Like, why would there be a ghost in my fridge?
jokes.setup: Joey defending his Pyramid performance
jokes.punchline: His continued confusion about his own ridiculous 'ghost' answer
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Joey still doesn't understand why his answer was wrong.

jokes.text: It's been 16 years, but the air quotes still hurt.
jokes.setup: Missy referring to his 'band' with air quotes
jokes.punchline: Ross's lingering sensitivity about their musical credibility
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Ross's enduring insecurity. Very relatable and well-crafted.

jokes.text: Well, Chandler and I used to make out. A lot.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining the pact he and Chandler made about not dating Missy
jokes.punchline: Missy revealing she and Chandler were already together
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that completely undermines the pact backstory. Great structure.

jokes.text: And on my turf?!
jokes.setup: Learning about Chandler and Missy making out in the science lab
jokes.punchline: Ross's outrage about them using 'his' space
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific reaction showing Ross's territorial nature about the science lab.

jokes.text: That's a lot of stairs.
jokes.setup: Expecting a sexy, fit stripper entrance
jokes.punchline: An out-of-shape older man complaining about the stairs
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of stripper expectations. Simple but effective contrast.

jokes.text: I should not have had that Mexican food for lunch.
jokes.setup: The aging stripper is clearly struggling
jokes.punchline: His TMI complaint about digestive issues
jokes.explanation: Adds to the uncomfortable reality of the situation. Functional cringe humor.

jokes.text: Officer... - Goodbody. - If you say so.
jokes.setup: The stripper introduces himself as 'Officer Goodbody'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic response clearly indicating his body is not good
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and perfect sarcastic delivery. Visual gag implied effectively.

jokes.text: We didn't know you wanted a stripper, so we got the phone book... and got the first name we could find. - How old's your phone book?
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining how they found this particular stripper
jokes.punchline: Monica's question implying they found him in a very old directory
jokes.explanation: Great structure and implication that he's been doing this job for decades.

jokes.text: You big piece of eye candy.
jokes.setup: Monica trying to cover her obvious disappointment with the stripper
jokes.punchline: Her transparently false compliment about his attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Monica's uncomfortable politeness.

jokes.text: She cringed. This is how I look when I'm turned-on.
jokes.setup: The stripper notices Monica's negative reaction
jokes.punchline: Monica's desperate lie about her facial expression
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the uncomfortable situation with Monica's desperation.

jokes.text: Shocking.
jokes.setup: The stripper saying he's been in the business for a long time
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic response indicating this is obvious
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy with good sarcastic delivery. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: And it's not like I can take them two at a time.
jokes.setup: The stripper complaining about climbing stairs
jokes.punchline: His admission about his physical limitations
jokes.explanation: Adds to the pathetic nature of the character. Solid supporting detail.

jokes.text: dance around in what I can only assume is a child's Halloween costume.
jokes.setup: Rachel criticizing the stripper's performance
jokes.punchline: Her specific insult about his costume being from a child
jokes.explanation: Harsh but specific insult that adds vivid imagery to the scene.

jokes.text: I may have borrowed this from my nephew... but let me assure you, what's underneath is all man.
jokes.setup: Defending his costume
jokes.punchline: His admission it's borrowed from a child and gross overconfidence about his body
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the pathetic situation with delusional confidence.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, did you say 'all man' or 'old man'?
jokes.setup: The stripper's claim about being 'all man'
jokes.punchline: Rachel's wordplay emphasizing his age
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with great economy. Sharp and effective put-down.

jokes.text: Big surprise. The hunk of beef has feelings.
jokes.setup: The stripper getting emotional about their criticism
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic response using objectifying language ironically
jokes.explanation: Good ironic use of typical stripper terminology. Solid sarcasm.

jokes.text: My friend Rachel has a kid. I totally know nursery rhymes.
jokes.setup: Joey getting a category about nursery rhymes
jokes.punchline: His misunderstanding about Congress vs. nursery rhymes and false confidence
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic and misplaced confidence. Shows his pattern of missing the point.

jokes.text: The thing hanging in your throat. - Uvula. Then pass.
jokes.setup: Joey trying to give a clue about Congressional terms
jokes.punchline: Henrietta's anatomically correct but completely wrong answer, Joey's quick pivot
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with unexpected anatomical knowledge. Great structure.

jokes.text: Excuse me. That didn't make us sound gay at all.
jokes.setup: Ross and Chandler arguing about promises to each other
jokes.punchline: Ross's self-aware comment about how their conversation sounds
jokes.explanation: Good meta-awareness but feels a bit dated. Decent character moment.

jokes.text: I believe the foundation of our friendship was unfortunate hair.
jokes.setup: Ross claiming their pact was the foundation of their friendship
jokes.punchline: Chandler's more realistic assessment of what bonded them
jokes.explanation: Excellent line with great economy and character insight. Very quotable.

jokes.text: It's really nice to see you. Not!
jokes.setup: College Monica talking to Chandler
jokes.punchline: Using the period-appropriate 'Not!' catchphrase after a false compliment
jokes.explanation: Good period humor showing young Monica's directness with dated slang.

jokes.text: Do not let me sit in one of those. We'll be here for days.
jokes.setup: Monica seeing beanbag chairs at the party
jokes.punchline: Her fear about getting stuck due to her weight at the time
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy reference that fits character history.

jokes.text: Oh, believe me, Ross, I won't be telling anybody about this.
jokes.setup: Ross asking Adrienne to keep their encounter secret
jokes.punchline: Her disgusted response indicating it was terrible for her
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Ross's romantic inexperience in college.

jokes.text: That's when I wrote the song 'Betrayal in the Common Room.'
jokes.setup: Ross explaining how hurt he was by Chandler's betrayal
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic, specific song title response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ross dramatic overreaction with specific, funny song title. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: What did you do to my mom?
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he wanted to get back at Ross by going after who Ross liked most
jokes.punchline: Ross's horrified assumption about his mother
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with Ross jumping to the wrong conclusion. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: For your information, ass-munch... I've lost four pounds. Maybe even five with all the dancing.
jokes.setup: Drunk college Monica confronting Chandler
jokes.punchline: Her aggressive weight loss update with crude name-calling
jokes.explanation: Good drunk college Monica moment showing her weight obsession and combativeness.

jokes.text: I am so not gonna do good on my SATs tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Drunk high school Rachel at the college party
jokes.punchline: Her concern about test performance while obviously intoxicated
jokes.explanation: Good young Rachel moment showing her priorities and naivety about drinking effects.

jokes.text: What a line.
jokes.setup: Chandler suggesting she could get a beauty scholarship
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic response to what she recognizes as a pickup line
jokes.explanation: Good economy showing Rachel seeing through Chandler's attempt at charm.

jokes.text: I should have hung up that breakaway jockstrap years ago.
jokes.setup: The stripper having an emotional breakdown about his career
jokes.punchline: His specific reference to professional stripper equipment
jokes.explanation: Good specificity that adds authenticity to the character's profession.

jokes.text: I can make my pecs dance. I can pick up a dollar bill with my butt cheeks.
jokes.setup: Trying to think of skills he has beyond stripping
jokes.punchline: His 'skills' are all still stripper-related physical tricks
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list of inappropriate 'skills' that miss the point entirely.

jokes.text: So maybe something in an office.
jokes.setup: The stripper listing his very physical, inappropriate skills
jokes.punchline: Monica's completely inappropriate career suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response with excellent timing. Great contrast with his skills.

jokes.text: I don't think my mom would mind.
jokes.setup: Discussing teaching stripping out of his apartment
jokes.punchline: Revealing he still lives with his mother
jokes.explanation: Adds another layer to the pathetic character. Solid character development.

jokes.text: You never know when it's gonna be your last dance.
jokes.setup: The stripper reflecting on his career ending
jokes.punchline: His unexpectedly philosophical moment about stripping
jokes.explanation: Gives unexpected depth to the character while maintaining humor.

jokes.text: Oak. Maple. Elm. Birch. I don't know. Types of trees...?
jokes.setup: Gene giving perfect clues for 'types of trees'
jokes.punchline: Joey's uncertainty even with obvious clues
jokes.explanation: Good Joey stupidity moment. Even with perfect clues he's uncertain.

jokes.text: Buenos días. Enchilada. Por favor. Oh, I'm so sorry, I don't know any Spanish words.
jokes.setup: Joey giving Spanish words as clues
jokes.punchline: Gene's response that he doesn't know Spanish, despite Joey just using Spanish words
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Gene not recognizing the obvious Spanish words Joey just said.

jokes.text: Dude. Dude, I think you're losing it. 'I have fur.' 'I like to bark.' What a dog says.
jokes.setup: Joey giving increasingly bizarre clues
jokes.punchline: Gene correctly interpreting Joey's strange clues as 'what a dog says'
jokes.explanation: Great Joey absurdist moment with Gene actually understanding despite the weirdness.

jokes.text: Girls Chandler could never get? - Supermodels.
jokes.setup: Joey listing supermodels as clues
jokes.punchline: Gene's interpretation as girls Chandler could never get
jokes.explanation: Good callback that works in the game show context while ribbing Chandler.

jokes.text: But it was, like, a million years ago. - It doesn't matter. - Why? - Because the night you kissed Rachel... was the night I kissed Rachel for the very first time.
jokes.setup: Ross dismissing the Chandler-Rachel kiss as ancient history
jokes.punchline: Monica revealing it was the same night as Ross's first kiss with Rachel
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that changes the stakes completely. Great structure building to revelation.

jokes.text: Oh, I thought she became a slut after she got her nose fixed.
jokes.setup: Learning Rachel kissed two guys in one night
jokes.punchline: Monica's harsh judgment with specific timeline reference
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment showing Monica's judgmental nature and knowledge of Rachel's history.

jokes.text: No! No! Yes! - You were under the pile of coats? - I was the pile of coats!
jokes.setup: Realizing there was confusion about which bed Rachel was on
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Chandler was buried under coats and got kissed by mistake
jokes.explanation: Brilliant revelation that recontextualizes everything. Perfect structure and incredibly funny twist.

jokes.text: You were my midnight mystery kisser?! You were my first kiss with Rachel?! You were my first kiss ever?!
jokes.setup: The realization about the mistaken kiss
jokes.punchline: Chandler's escalating realizations culminating in it being his first kiss ever
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with each revelation getting more significant. Excellent structure and payoff.

jokes.text: What did I marry into?!
jokes.setup: The revelation about Ross and Chandler's accidental kiss
jokes.punchline: Monica's repeated horror about her husband's history with her brother
jokes.explanation: Good callback that bookends the episode's revelations about her husband and brother.
